title: Wide Op w/ no temp when using interfaces with --public-flat-rw
body:
Can you attach an example that shows the issue? 

```
localparam integer WIDTH = 65; // Works when set to 64 or less                                       
interface intf #() ();                                                          
    logic  [WIDTH-1:0]  tdata ;                                                 
endinterface : intf                                                             
                                                                                
module tb_transposilator;                                                       
                                                                                
logic clk;                                                                      
                                                                                
initial                                                                         
begin                                                                           
    clk = 1'b1;                                                                 
                                                                                
    #20;                                                                        
                                                                                
    forever                                                                     
    begin                                                                       
        #5; clk = ~clk;                                                         
    end                                                                         
end                                                                             
                                                                                
// logic [127:0] dat_in_tdata;                                                  
// logic [127:0] cmd_tdata;                                                     
                                                                                
intf #( ) cmd();                                                                
intf #( ) dat_in();                                                             
                                                                                    
int ii = 0;                                                                         
                                                                                    
always begin                                                                        
    @(posedge clk);                                                             
                                                                                
    dat_in.tdata[7: 0] <= ii > 0 ? '1 : '0;                                         
    // uncommenting this seems to also fix these issues.                                                                
    // cmd.tdata [WIDTH-1:   0] <= '0; // WORKS                                     
    cmd.tdata [WIDTH-1:   0] <= cmd.tdata [WIDTH-1:0] ^ '1;                         
end                                                                             
                                                                                
endmodule                                                                       
```

I tried to get this down to as minimal example as I could; a lot of the weirdness seems necessary. I haven't tried this particular example in another simulator but at a minimum I'd expect a failure mode that's less opaque. Originally this error exhibited when the two statements were in separate always blocks but this seems simpler.

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
`verilator --lint-only --timing --public-flat-rw ./replicate2.sv`

Produces the following output:
```
%Error: Internal Error: replicate2.sv:36:55: ../V3EmitCFunc.cpp:105: Wide Op w/ no temp, perhaps missing op in V3EmitC?
   36 |     cmd.tdata [WIDTH-1:   0] <= cmd.tdata [WIDTH-1:0] ^ '1;
      |                                                       ^
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
`Verilator 5.039 devel rev v5.038-164-g60cbbf0ec`
The error does not reproduce on the following version `Verilator 5.020 2024-01-01 rev (Debian 5.020-1)`

I see some warnings printed when building about bison versions but they seemed not relevant.

What OS and distribution are you using?
Ubuntu 24.04 and Ubuntu 20.04 LTS

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
I'm probably a bit out of my depth but I could try a little bit.
code_blocks:
